title: Assurance Evidence Gate Model Investigation
goal: Answer GitHub #1131 by mapping existing AW assurance, proof, Planning, Memory, report, and closeout building blocks into one repo-defined evidence-gate model.
non_goals: Do not implement new config schema, commands, proof behavior, closeout gates, or fixtures in this investigation slice.
Do not encode domain-specific compliance knowledge in AW.
Do not create a parallel compliance subsystem.

review_mode.review question: How should repo-defined assurance requirements compose with existing AW surfaces without becoming a separate compliance product?

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --task "Ingest the #1130 lane on a new branch and do the #1131 investigation" --format json; gh issue view 1130 --json number,title,state,body,labels,assignees,comments,url; gh issue view 1131 --json number,title,state,body,labels,assignees,comments,url; uv run agentic-workspace planning new-plan --id github-1131-assurance-evidence-gate-investigation --title "Investigate assurance evidence gate model" --source "GitHub #1131; parent lane GitHub #1130" --target . --activate --format json; rg assurance/proof/workflow/closeout surfaces; targeted source reads of config, workflow obligations, proof assurance, closeout, and config-effect review.

findings.title: Existing surfaces already form most of the assurance loop
findings.summary: AW already has repo assurance config, workflow obligations, Planning assurance fields, proof-profile integration, closeout completion options, Memory residue routing, and ADR authority routing. The first implementation should compose these rather than add a compliance subsystem.
findings.evidence: config.py parses assurance.default_level, strict_closeout, proof_profiles, test_data_policy, invariant_registry, risk_registry, and workflow_obligations; planning-execplan.schema.json includes adaptive_assurance, traceability_refs, control_gates, test_data_policy, risk_registry_refs, invariant_refs; proof selection reads active planning assurance; closeout_trust already emits completion_options.
findings.risk if unchanged: A broad new subsystem would duplicate existing surfaces, increase ordinary-work cost, and weaken the repo-native boundary #1130 is trying to preserve.
findings.suggested action: Add a small requirement fact model under [assurance.requirements.<id>] and project it into existing assurance/proof/Planning/closeout surfaces.
findings.promotion target: follow-up issue: assurance requirement fact model
findings.post-remediation note shape: Keep as review evidence until child issues exist; no Memory note needed.

findings.title: Minimal v1 requirement shape should be declarative and domain-generic
findings.summary: The smallest stable v1 shape should capture matching, authority refs, required evidence, proof/review linkage, force, and claim blockers. Domain meaning stays in repo-owned authority refs and free-form evidence labels.
findings.evidence: #1130 proposed id, level, applies_to_paths, applies_to_task_markers, authority_refs, required_evidence, proof_profile, workflow_obligation_refs, review_owner, force, blocking_claims; config.py already supports assurance levels and proof profiles; workflow obligations already support force/stage/commands/review_hint.
findings.risk if unchanged: Over-typing evidence or domain semantics would make AW appear to certify compliance; under-typing would leave agents unable to gate claims.
findings.suggested action: Use required fields id/level/at least one applies_to selector/force plus optional authority_refs, required_evidence, proof_profile, workflow_obligation_refs, review_owner, blocking_claims, notes.
findings.promotion target: follow-up issue: assurance requirement fact model
findings.post-remediation note shape: Retain in child issue acceptance, then shrink review.

findings.title: Evidence status should be a projection first, not a new evidence store
findings.summary: Active evidence should live in Planning/proof/closeout fields for planned work and in explicit proof/closeout records for direct work. Memory is only for durable lessons or unresolved reusable residue.
findings.evidence: Planning records already carry proof_report, intent_proof, control_gates evidence, traceability_refs, durable_residue, closeout_distillation, and task_intent_promotion. closeout_trust already reconciles proof, intent, acceptance, package evidence, and residue. Memory index says Memory is anti-rediscovery, not active task state.
findings.risk if unchanged: A new evidence store would compete with Planning/proof ownership and create another state surface agents must reconcile.
findings.suggested action: Define an assurance_evidence_status projection with requirement_id, state, evidence_present, missing_evidence, sources, blocking_claims, and next_action. Read from existing fields first; add a command-owned writer only if direct-work evidence cannot be represented cleanly.
findings.promotion target: follow-up issue: evidence status and closeout gates
findings.post-remediation note shape: No Memory note; active evidence ownership belongs to Planning/proof/closeout.

findings.title: Closeout should block broad claims, not all progress
findings.summary: Missing high/critical assurance evidence should block claim-work-complete and close-parent-lane by default; claim-slice-complete can remain allowed only when the slice explicitly excludes the missing evidence or routes continuation honestly.
findings.evidence: _closeout_completion_options already distinguishes claim-slice-complete, claim-work-complete, close-parent-lane, route-residue, request-review, and stop-with-status. Tests already verify work-claim blocking for weak intent proof and closeout residue.
findings.risk if unchanged: Agents may treat passing tests as compliance/evidence satisfaction, or the product may overcorrect by blocking honest partial progress.
findings.suggested action: Feed assurance_evidence_status into existing completion option blockers: high/critical missing required evidence blocks work/parent claims; blocking force can also block slice claim unless explicitly scoped out; request-review and stop-with-status remain available.
findings.post-remediation note shape: Child issue acceptance should require both blocked broad claims and allowed honest status stops.

findings.title: Proportionality depends on strict matching and quiet defaults
findings.summary: Configured high-assurance rules must not make unrelated work noisy. A requirement should activate only from explicit path glob match, task marker match, active Planning reference, proof profile reference, or risk/invariant reference.
findings.evidence: workflow_obligations currently matches from scope tags derived from active planning, task text, and changed paths; proof selection only enriches with configured assurance profiles when active planning assurance declares them.
findings.risk if unchanged: Repos with assurance config could make every small typo or README edit feel like regulated work, undermining AW's small-work ergonomics.
findings.suggested action: Require match evidence per active requirement and emit quiet status for non-matches. Tests must include a high-assurance matched task and an unrelated small edit that does not emit active requirements.
findings.post-remediation note shape: Keep proportionality as a guardrail in every child issue.

promote: Create a child issue for the minimal [assurance.requirements.<id>] fact model in config/schema/defaults/config output.
Create a child issue for matching/projection into start, implement, and report with high-assurance and quiet-low-assurance tests.
Create a child issue for evidence status and closeout completion-option gates.
Create a child issue for proof-profile integration from matched requirements.
Create a child issue for high-assurance and non-code dogfood fixtures after the first implementation slices exist.
defer: Any new evidence store until Planning/proof/closeout projection proves insufficient.
Any new top-level command until selectors/report sections prove too weak.
dismiss: Blocking all work in a repo merely because assurance requirements are configured.

retention.proof surface: routed issues, planning state, docs, checks, Memory, or a compact retained review stub

review_finding.rule: Use only these headings when a prose finding is needed; keep the canonical fields structured.

field_map.Next owner: execution_summary/follow-on routed to or required_continuation/owner surface

handoff_or_closeout.rule: Prefer structured fields; use this shape only for short human-readable summaries.

summary.recommended_model: Repo declares assurance requirements under [assurance.requirements.<id>]. AW matches requirements by explicit repo-owned signals, projects authority/evidence/proof/review facts into existing surfaces, and feeds missing evidence into existing completion options.

surface_integration.start: compactly report matched task-marker requirements and authority refs only when task text activates a requirement
surface_integration.implement: match changed paths plus task markers; expose active requirements behind selector/verbose and keep tiny output quiet unless required action changes
surface_integration.proof: include requirement-linked proof profiles, review aids, blocked commands, and residual missing evidence
surface_integration.closeout_trust: summarize evidence status and add blockers to existing completion options
surface_integration.workflow_obligations: allow requirements to reference or synthesize obligations rather than duplicate staged commands/reviews
surface_integration.planning: carry requirement refs, evidence status, proof profile, review owner, and claim boundary in existing execplan fields
surface_integration.memory: capture only durable lessons or unresolved assurance residue, not active evidence

non_goals: high-assurance ceremony for unrelated low-assurance work

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
drift_log: 2026-05-26: Filled with #1131 assurance evidence-gate investigation and follow-up slice recommendations.
